step_name: Identify AI use cases and risk classification

subprocess_flow.description: Collect and catalog all incoming AI project proposals from various business units. This involves reviewing the initial project documentation to understand the intended purpose, target users, and technical architecture of the proposed AI system.
success_criteria: All active AI proposals are captured in the central inventory.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To create a comprehensive list of AI systems requiring assessment.
subprocess_flow.scope: All new and significantly modified AI projects within the organization.
triggers: Initiation of a new AI project.
end_conditions: Inventory is updated with the latest project details.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Shadow AI projects bypass the inventory process.

subprocess_flow.description: Evaluate each AI use case against the list of prohibited practices defined by the EU AI Act, such as social scoring or untargeted scraping of facial images. This step ensures that no project proceeds if it violates fundamental ethical or legal boundaries.
inputs: EU AI Act Prohibited Practices List
success_criteria: Confirmation that the use case does not fall under prohibited categories.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To prevent the development of illegal AI systems.
end_conditions: Screening report is signed off by legal counsel.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Legal department review of all screening reports.

governance_requirements: EU AI Act Title II (Prohibited AI Practices)

change_management.versioning_rules: Screening criteria updated with regulatory changes.

subprocess_flow.substep_name: Apply EU AI Act Risk Criteria
subprocess_flow.description: Assess the AI system against the high-risk criteria specified in the EU AI Act, focusing on its application in critical infrastructure, education, employment, and financial services. This involves determining if the system is a safety component or intended for use in sensitive sectors.
inputs: EU AI Act Risk Classification Guidelines
success_criteria: Risk level (Unacceptable, High, Limited, Minimal) is assigned based on regulatory criteria.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To categorize the AI system for regulatory compliance mapping.
triggers: Successful completion of prohibited practice screening.
end_conditions: Draft classification is ready for executive review.
governance_requirements: EU AI Act Annex III (High-Risk AI Systems)

subprocess_flow.description: Compile the findings from the inventory, screening, and classification steps into a formal Risk Classification Report. This report serves as the definitive document for the project's regulatory standing and dictates the subsequent compliance requirements.
dependencies: Apply EU AI Act Risk Criteria
success_criteria: Formal approval of risk classification by Chief Compliance Officer.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To document the final risk standing of the AI system.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Incomplete documentation in the final report.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Peer review of the report before submission.
